gestures.name: GT-D
gestures.description: Deictic (지시적): a fingertip or hand extends toward a specific direction or target and holds (pointing).

gestures.name: GT-I
gestures.description: Iconic (상징적): a circular or curved path that imitates the shape or form of a concrete object.

gestures.name: GT-M
gestures.description: Metaphoric (은유적): gives spatial form/motion to an ABSTRACT idea you can name — e.g. weighing two options like scales, placing past/future or choices left vs right, a rising motion for 'increase', cupped hands as an abstract container. STRICT: do NOT use for plain directional motion that is only rhythmic emphasis (GT-B), for pointing (GT-D), for tracing a concrete object's real shape (GT-I), or for incidental motion. If you cannot state the specific abstract meaning, do not code GT-M.

gestures.name: GT-B
gestures.description: Beat (박자적): short, repeated movements with a steady rhythm, in time with speech (emphasis, not meaning).

gestures.name: GT-E
gestures.description: Emblematic (관습적): a culturally standardized conventional pattern (e.g., raising a hand, thumbs-up, OK sign).

gestures.name: GT-X
gestures.description: Unclassifiable (판별 불가): hand or arm movement is present but its gesture type cannot be determined.
